geo_signal_seed.title: Peak Tourism Starts Moving Around Heat Risk
geo_signal_seed.summary: Heatwaves, water pressure, wildfire risk, and booking behavior point toward tourism value shifting toward cooler timing and better-adapted destinations.
geo_signal_seed.implication: Destinations with shade, water reliability, emergency planning, and shoulder-season appeal gain relative advantage.

reference_class_prior.label: climate comfort changing consumer timing before destination demand disappears
reference_class_prior.rationale: Initial scaffold prior for later PFE scoring; replace with measured priors as resolution history accumulates.

leading_indicators.text: tour operators changing peak-season schedules
hotels investing in cooling and water resilience
municipal visitor caps or wildfire warnings affecting booking windows

disconfirmers.text: bookings stay concentrated in peak heat despite repeated warnings
wildfire and water disruptions fall for multiple seasons

candidates.title: Care Automation Moves Into Mundane Infrastructure
candidates.summary: Ageing, caregiver scarcity, sensors, and reimbursement pressure make practical care automation more important than theatrical robots.
candidates.implication: Useful adoption may concentrate in lifts, fall prevention, scheduling, and ambient monitoring before humanoid systems.
